Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Marker-assisted selection for benzoxazinoid profiles to improve pest/disease resistance; tropical vs temperate alleles confer different defensive metabolite profiles
Genes.Chromosome_Position: Chromosome 1, 110-kb region containing BX10, BX11, BX12 paralogs
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: GWAS confirmed Bx10-12 locus on chromosome 1 as a major QTL for HDMBOA-Glc abundance; this locus is also a metabolite QTL hotspot controlling multiple benzoxazinoid mass features in both leaf tips and bases
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS mapping with 29 million SNPs; confirmation of known biosynthetic genes; haplotype-based additive effect analysis
Genes.Developmental_Stage: Seedling stage (third leaf visibly emerged from whorl)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: O-methyltransferases catalyzing biosynthesis of HDMBOA-Glc from DIMBOA-Glc
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Tropical haplotype associated with higher HDMBOA-Glc; temperate haplotype associated with higher DIMBOA-Glc
Genes.Field_Trials_or_GxE: Greenhouse-grown under natural sunlight, single location (Ithaca, NY), early June
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Controls methylation of DIMBOA-Glc to HDMBOA-Glc; natural variation at this locus determines the ratio of these two benzoxazinoids across maize subpopulations
Genes.Genetic_Population: Goodman Diversity Panel (282 maize inbred lines, 5 subpopulations)
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: SNPs in LD with Bx10-12 locus plus additional cis-regulatory SNPs in adjacent LD blocks
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: This locus corresponds to the metabolite QTL hotspot on chromosome 1 found in both leaf tips and bases. Several non-benzoxazinoid mass features also mapped to this locus, suggesting indirect regulation of other metabolite classes.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: Nontargeted UPLC-HRMS (Q Exactive mass spectrometer), negative electron spray ionization
Genes.Nutrient_Trait_Metric: content; concentration (signal intensity normalized by tissue weight and total ion concentration)
Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with 29M SNPs (Bukowski et al., 2018); transcriptomics (Kremling et al., 2018)
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Benzoxazinoid degradation has been shown to induce other maize defense responses
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Tropical maize lines accumulate significantly more HDMBOA-Glc and less DIMBOA-Glc than temperate lines (P<0.05, ANOVA)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS confirmed Bx10-12 on chromosome 1 as a major locus controlling HDMBOA-Glc abundance in maize seedling leaves. 2. This locus is a metabolite QTL hotspot affecting multiple benzoxazinoid compounds. 3. Tropical maize lines carry alleles favoring HDMBOA-Glc accumulation while temperate lines favor DIMBOA-Glc. 4. Additive effects with a novel chromosome 9 locus further modulate HDMBOA-Glc content.
Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Nutrient_Accumulation: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases (third leaf)
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Normalized MS signal intensity, FW basis

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: GWAS confirmed Bx13 on chromosome 2 as a QTL for DIM₂BOA-Glc abundance, with most significantly associated SNPs in LD with the biosynthetic gene
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GWAS (confirmation of previously known locus from Handrick et al., 2016)
Genes.Genetic_Population: Goodman Diversity Panel (282 maize inbred lines)
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GWAS validation of a previously reported biosynthetic gene.
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS confirmed Bx13 on chromosome 2 as a known QTL controlling DIM₂BOA-Glc abundance. 2. Most significantly associated SNPs are in LD with the Bx13 biosynthetic gene. 3. Additional SNPs in adjacent LD blocks suggest potential cis-regulatory loci.
Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Nutrient_Accumulation: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Normalized MS signal intensity, FW basis

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Novel marker for HDMBOA-Glc content; potential stacking with Bx10-12 alleles for additive improvement of benzoxazinoid defense
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: A novel GWAS locus on chromosome 9 with additive effect on HDMBOA-Glc content; the 20 inbred lines with highest GRMZM2G108309 expression had significantly different HDMBOA-Glc content compared to the 20 with lowest expression
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS mapping; haplotype-based additive effect analysis with Bx10-12; expression-metabolite correlation analysis (top/bottom 20 lines)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Predicted protein phosphatase 2C family protein; novel locus affecting HDMBOA-Glc regulation
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expression levels significantly different between inbred lines carrying different alleles of the 25-kb linkage block on chromosome 9
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Haplotype associated with higher GRMZM2G108309 expression correlates with higher HDMBOA-Glc
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Regulates HDMBOA-Glc accumulation; additive effect with Bx10-12 locus on chromosome 1
Genes.Genetic_Population: Goodman Diversity Panel (282 maize inbred lines)
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: SNPs in a 25-kb LD block on chromosome 9, located immediately 3' of GRMZM2G108309
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: This locus was not identified in prior biparental mapping studies (Meihls et al., 2013; Zheng et al., 2015; Handrick et al., 2016), highlighting the power of broader genetic diversity panels. Correlation between expression and HDMBOA-Glc was significant only when comparing extreme expression groups (top/bottom 20), not across the entire population. The associated SNPs are downstream of the gene, and the causative locus may not be present in B73.
Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with 29M SNPs; transcriptomics (Kremling et al., 2018)
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Significant difference in HDMBOA-Glc between high and low expression groups (P<0.05); additive effect with Bx10-12 locus
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Possibly 3' regulatory region affecting gene expression; expression level correlates with HDMBOA-Glc content in extreme lines but not across the full population
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified a novel locus on chromosome 9 associated with HDMBOA-Glc abundance. 2. The candidate gene GRMZM2G108309 encodes a predicted protein phosphatase 2C. 3. Haplotypic segregation at this locus significantly affects gene expression. 4. Additive effect with Bx10-12 on chromosome 1 for HDMBOA-Glc content. 5. The 20 lines with highest vs lowest expression showed significant HDMBOA-Glc differences.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Normalized MS signal intensity, FW basis

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Marker for phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid ester isomeric variation; potential for selecting specific defensive metabolite profiles in tropical vs temperate germplasm
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Coding-sequence variation in GRMZM2G063909 controls isomeric variation of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters; the two isomer groups are mutually exclusive across maize genotypes and perfectly cosegregate with genotypic markers at this locus in near-isogenic lines
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS mapping; NMR structural confirmation of metabolites; cosegregation in B73×Ki11 and B73×CML247 near-isogenic lines (RIL-derived); MS phenotyping of NIL segregants
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Citrate synthase-like enzyme; ortholog of Arabidopsis and rice citrate synthase genes; likely catalyzes formation of hydroxycitric acid moiety conjugated with phenylpropanoids
Genes.Experimental_Materials: 282-line Goodman Diversity Panel; B73×Ki11 and B73×CML247 6th-generation RIL-derived near-isogenic lines
Genes.Expression_Pattern: No significant difference in GRMZM2G063909 expression between maize lines accumulating different isomers in either leaf tips or bases, suggesting structural/coding variation rather than expression-level regulation
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Temperate allele (B73-type) produces 2-O-acylated (more polar) isomers; tropical allele (Ki11/CML247-type) produces less polar isomers (likely 3-O-acylated). Both may have defensive functions.
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters (coumaroyl-, caffeoyl-, feruloyl-hydroxycitric acid conjugates)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Coding-sequence variation determines which structural isomers of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid esters are produced; temperate allele produces more polar 2-O-acylated isomers, tropical allele produces less polar isomers (likely 3-O-acylated)
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GWAS (novel gene discovery); metabolite QTL hotspot identification on chromosome 4
Genes.Genetic_Population: Goodman Diversity Panel (282 lines); B73×Ki11 RILs; B73×CML247 RILs (nested association mapping population)
Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid ester accumulation is induced by Pseudomonas putida (cited from Plenchamp, 2013)
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: All linked SNPs are in the coding region of GRMZM2G063909
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: This locus corresponds to the metabolite QTL hotspot on chromosome 4 found in both leaf tips and bases. The less polar (tropical) isomers were unstable and degraded during NMR analysis, preventing full structural elucidation. Authors hypothesize these are 3-O-acylated hydroxycitric acid esters. Heterozygous NILs showed codominant/intermediate phenotype producing both isomers at reduced levels. The isomers have mutually exclusive distribution patterns across the diversity panel, with rare isomers overrepresented in tropical lines.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: Nontargeted UPLC-HRMS (negative ESI); 2D-NMR (1H, COSY, HSQC, HMBC) for structural confirmation
Genes.Nutrient_Trait_Metric: content; isomeric ratio (presence/absence of specific structural isomers)
Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with 29M SNPs; transcriptomics (Kremling et al., 2018); NMR spectroscopy
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Potential defensive function; accumulation induced by soil bacterium Pseudomonas putida
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Perfect cosegregation between genotype and isomer class in NILs; heterozygotes produce both isomers at intermediate levels; presence/absence of isomers is essentially binary
Genes.Reaction_Product_or_Immediate_Output: Coumaroyl hydroxycitric acid, caffeoyl hydroxycitric acid, feruloyl hydroxycitric acid (2-O-acylated or 3-O-acylated isomers)
Genes.Reaction_Substrate: Coumaric acid, caffeic acid, ferulic acid, hydroxycitric acid
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Structural/coding variation in the enzyme rather than expression-level regulation (no expression difference between allele groups)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS mapped all three pairs of phenylpropanoid hydroxycitric acid ester isomers to a single 10-kb LD block on chromosome 4 containing GRMZM2G063909. 2. GRMZM2G063909 encodes a citrate synthase-like enzyme, consistent with the hydroxycitric acid moiety of the metabolites. 3. Coding-sequence (not expression) variation determines which structural isomers are produced. 4. Perfect cosegregation confirmed in B73×Ki11 and B73×CML247 near-isogenic lines. 5. Heterozygotes produce both isomers at intermediate abundance (codominant). 6. Tropical maize lines preferentially accumulate less polar isomers while temperate lines accumulate more polar 2-O-acylated isomers.
Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Nutrient_Accumulation: Seedling leaf tips and leaf bases
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Normalized MS signal intensity (normalized by tissue FW and total ion concentration)
Genes.Variety: B73, Ki11, CML247, and derived NILs

Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Differential expression of chalcone synthase genes between leaf tips and bases explains the tissue-specific presence/absence of flavonoids; flavonoids are completely absent in leaf base metabolome
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomic analysis (Kremling et al., 2018) showing differential expression between tissues; metabolomic profiling showing flavonoid presence/absence
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Chalcone synthase; catalyzes the first committed step in flavonoid biosynthesis
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Significantly higher expression in leaf tips than leaf bases (FDR<0.05); these are the two most strongly expressed of five chalcone synthase genes in B73 genome
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Higher expression in leaf tips correlates with flavonoid presence; flavonoids completely absent in leaf bases where expression is low
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate gene (known function); expression analysis from published transcriptomics
Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage/tissue position (leaf tip vs base)
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Five chalcone synthase genes exist in the B73 genome; only the two most highly expressed showed significant tissue-type differential expression. This is correlative evidence (expression + metabolite co-occurrence) rather than direct genetic perturbation. Flavonoid differentiation is the primary driver of tissue-type metabolome separation in PCA.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: UPLC-HRMS with UV absorbance (200-400 nm); RNA-seq transcriptomics
Genes.Nutrient_Trait_Metric: presence/absence of flavonoid peaks; gene expression level
Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (Kremling et al., 2018); nontargeted metabolomics (this study)
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Flavonoids likely serve defensive functions against biotic and abiotic stress in exposed leaf tips
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Flavonoid peaks completely absent in leaf bases vs present in leaf tips
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Developmental/tissue-specific transcriptional regulation; higher expression in more developmentally advanced leaf tips
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two chalcone synthase genes (GRMZM2G422750, GRMZM2G380650) are significantly more expressed in leaf tips than bases across the diversity panel. 2. This differential expression corresponds to the complete absence of flavonoid metabolites in leaf bases and their presence in leaf tips. 3. Flavonoid abundance is the major driver of metabolomic differentiation between tissue types (>30% variance in PCA).
Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Nutrient_Accumulation: Seedling leaf tips (flavonoids present); leaf bases (flavonoids absent)

Title: Metabolome-Scale Genome-Wide Association Studies Reveal Chemical Diversity and Genetic Control of Maize Specialized Metabolites
